This family comprises a large number of flowering plants, many of which are toxic or have medicinal properties. Uragoga dalzellii is further classified in the genus Uragoga, which is a small genus of flowering plants found primarily in the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The classification of Uragoga dalzellii reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Apocynaceae family.

intro: Uragoga dalzellii, also known as Kaempferia galanga or Thai galangal, is a type of plant in the ginger family (Zingiberaceae). The health benefits of Uragoga dalzellii include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to aid in digestion and lower blood sugar levels. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga dalzellii include kaempferol, galangin, and gingerols, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Uragoga dalzellii is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in cooking and as a flavoring agent in various Southeast Asian dishes.
